title: Public Internet Access Sites * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Public Internet Access Sites * Homeless Youth 8 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homeless Women Homeless Youth Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT TEEN DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers a food pantry, help applying for state benefits such as OHP and SNAP, showers, laundry facilities. Also offers assistance with educational needs such as re-enrollment, school supplies, and specialized information and referral. Eligibility: Ages 11-24 that are homeless and unaccompanied by an adult Hours: Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm Email (800) 660-0934 Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A drop in center that provides basic needs services such as meals, recreation/activities, showers, laundry, phone, mailing address, and computers. Also offers a safe space for LGBTQ youth. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are experiencing homelessness and are eligible to enter the Homeless Youth Continuum Hours: Drop-In: Tuesday/Thursday/Sunday 1pm-6pm Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10am Lunch: Sunday-Friday 1pm-2pm Dinner: Sunday-Friday 5pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY HOME YOUTH SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM
